title: GRIP: In-Parameter Graph Reasoning through Fine-Tuning Large Language Models

method.name: GRIP (Graph Reasoning In-Parameterization)
method.description: A framework that equips LLMs with the ability to internalize complex relational information from graphs through carefully designed fine-tuning tasks
key_features: Knowledge is stored within lightweight LoRA parameters
Fine-tuned LLM can perform graph-related tasks without requiring access to the original graph at inference time
Inspired by in-parameter knowledge injection for test-time adaptation of LLMs

introduction.context: Large Language Models (LLMs) like ChatGPT and DeepSeek have revolutionized AI by demonstrating remarkable generalization capabilities across various downstream tasks
introduction.challenge: Tasks involving intricate data structures like graphs are difficult to leverage with LLMs

limitations: Token overhead and degraded inference performance
Overhead scales linearly with number of queries
Large graphs can exceed LLM context window
Limitations in solving graph tasks under node permutation
Requires careful model design and alignment between language and graph modalities
Requires modifications to the model, preventing use of common acceleration techniques
